## 4. Does scoop `ruby` put `gem` on PATH? Existing gem/ruby handling?

**Existing handling:** There is **no** existing gem handling anywhere in the repo. `ruby` appears in exactly one install site — `Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')` at line 243, gated on `installExtraRuntimes`. No `gem`, `bundler`, `gem install`, `--user-install`, or GEM_HOME/GEM_PATH references exist in any `.tmpl`/`.ps1`/`.yml` (confirmed by grep; only mentions are in `.specstory/history/` transcripts and docs prose). `docs/tools.md:35` documents ruby only as one of the "extra runtimes" (`go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`).

**`gem` on PATH after scoop `ruby`:** Yes. Scoop's `ruby` manifest shims both `ruby.exe` and `gem` (a `gem.cmd`/`gem.bat`) into `~/scoop/shims`, which is already on PATH. So `Have gem` (the `Get-Command` check, line 41) will resolve in a fresh shell after install. Two caveats worth putting in the plan:
- Scoop may prompt/print a post-install notice suggesting `ridk install` (MSYS2 DevKit) for gems with native extensions. `try-cli` is a pure-Ruby single-file gem (no native build — see the `.specstory` research at `2026-07-12_08-49-45Z-gst-gp-git-alias.md`), so `gem install try-cli` should not need the DevKit.
- Within the same `chezmoi apply` run, if `ruby` was *just* installed by an earlier `Scoop-Install` in the same process, `~/scoop/shims` was already on PATH before the script started, so `gem` should be found; but scoop occasionally needs `scoop reset ruby` or a PATH refresh. The fault-tolerant `Register-Failure 'gem:try-cli'` fallback (retriable on next `just apply`) covers the rare miss — consistent with the `az`-not-yet-on-PATH precedent (lines 348–354).

I have everything I need. The installed gem is `try-cli-1.7.1` (not the target 1.9.3), which turned out to be useful: it confirms real cross-version drift I can cite. Here is the complete design.

---

# PowerShell 7 `try` fragment — design

## 0. Ground truth I verified (not from memory)

I read the actual `try.rb` emit helpers and generated byte-exact output on this Mac.

- **Wrapper contract** (`cmd_init!`, lines 1003-1013): the shell wrapper runs `ruby '<script>' exec [--path 'PATH'] "$@" 2>/dev/tty`, then `if exit==0: eval "$out" else: echo "$out"`. So **exit code is the signal**: `0` = emit-script to evaluate; non-zero = a human message (`Cancelled.`, error text) to print verbatim.
- **`exec` dispatch** (lines 1230-1279): sub-verb is `clone` / `worktree` / `cd`, else the first token is treated as a search query. `try . name` arrives as `exec . name` → falls through to `cmd_cd!` → `mkdir_cd` or `worktree`.
- **Emit format** (od-verified): a leading `#`-comment line, then commands joined by the exact 6-byte separator `SPACE & & SPACE \ \n` followed by a 2-space indent. First command has **no** indent.
- **Test hooks** (lines 910-913): the real flags are `--and-type`, `--and-exit`, `--and-keys`, `--and-confirm` (the selector's non-interactive drivers — key to Pester for delete/rename).
- **Version drift is real and load-bearing**: 1.7.1 has **no** `ascend`/`graduate`/`ln -s`/`git worktree move` (grep returned nothing), and its `script_delete` restore-trailer is `( cd 'PWD' 2>/dev/null || cd "$HOME" )` — a **subshell**, with a **double-quoted `"$HOME"`** fallback, **not** `cd 'BASE'` as the 1.9.3 spec in the brief says. This proves the translator must be marker-gated + verb-tolerant, and must proactively handle `ln`/`git worktree move`/`mv` even though the locally-installed gem never emits them.

Authentic emit samples I captured (these become Pester goldens):

```
# create (non-git cwd), exec --path /tmp/tries . myproj
mkdir -p '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-myproj' && \
  touch '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-myproj' && \
  echo '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-myproj' && \
  cd '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-myproj'

# clone
mkdir -p '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-tobi-try' && \
  echo 'Using git clone to create this trial from https://github.com/tobi/try.' && \
  git clone 'https://github.com/tobi/try' '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-tobi-try' && \
  touch '...' && echo '...' && cd '...'

# worktree (explicit)
mkdir -p '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-wt-name' && \
  echo 'Using git worktree ...' && \
  /usr/bin/env sh -c 'if git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then repo=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel); git -C "$repo" worktree add --detach '/tmp/tries/2026-07-12-wt-name' >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; exit 0' && \
  touch '...' && echo '...' && cd '...'
```

---

## 1. Fragment structure

Place at `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/32_try.ps1` (matches the Unix `32_try.zsh` number; loads after git/herdr, before `98_tv_cache`). The loader dot-sources it (`. $fragment.FullName`), so top-level vars/functions land in the session's global scope — `function try` will be callable and its `Set-Location` will move the *live* session.

Four internal helpers + one public function:

| symbol | role |
|---|---|
| `$script:TryScriptPath` (module-scope state) | resolved path to `try.rb` |
| `_Try-ParsePosixArgs` | POSIX single/double-quote tokenizer → `string[]` |
| `_Try-InvokeOne` | translate + run one command element; returns `$true`/`$false` for `&&` |
| `_Try-InvokeEmitted` | strip comment, split on the continuation separator, run elements with short-circuit |
| `try` | the public wrapper: run ruby, capture stdout, gate on marker+exit, dispatch |

**Scope caveat to flag in the plan:** `$script:` inside a dot-sourced fragment resolves to global, so `function try` reading `$script:TryScriptPath` works. If you want to be bulletproof against a future switch to a real module/`Import-Module`, use `$global:TryScriptPath` or bake the path into the function via `.GetNewClosure()`. I recommend `$script:` for consistency with the repo's other fragments, with a one-line comment noting the dependency on dot-sourcing.

---

## 2. Env setup + guard + cached script resolution

```powershell
# 32_try.ps1 — try-cli (ephemeral workspace manager) for pwsh.
# try's shell integration evals POSIX shell; pwsh can't. So instead of using
# `try init`'s (broken-on-pwsh) wrapper, we run `ruby try.rb exec` ourselves and
# translate its POSIX stdout into native pwsh in THIS session. See docs/…

# ── env (mirrors dot_config/zsh/tools/32_try.zsh:7-9) ────────────────────────
if (-not $env:TRY_PATH) { $env:TRY_PATH = Join-Path $HOME 'src/tries' }
# ${TRY_PATH:A}: absolutize always, resolve symlinks only if it already exists.
$env:TRY_PATH = [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($env:TRY_PATH)
try { $env:TRY_PATH = (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $env:TRY_PATH -ErrorAction Stop).Path } catch { }
if (-not $env:TRY_PROJECTS) { $env:TRY_PROJECTS = Split-Path -Parent $env:TRY_PATH }

# ── guard: opt-in tool. No ruby → inert (model: 25_herdr.ps1:32). ────────────
if (-not (Get-Command ruby -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { return }

# ── cached try.rb path (mirrors 32_try.zsh:16-28 mtime idiom + 98_tv_cache) ──
$script:TryScriptPath = $null
$cacheFile = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh/try_script.txt'
$rubyExe   = (Get-Command ruby).Source
$fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and
         ((Get-Item -LiteralPath $cacheFile).LastWriteTime -ge (Get-Item -LiteralPath $rubyExe).LastWriteTime)
if ($fresh) { $script:TryScriptPath = (Get-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Raw).Trim() }

if (-not $script:TryScriptPath -or -not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $script:TryScriptPath)) {
    # ~30-60ms ruby cold start; only on cache miss / ruby upgrade / gem move.
    $resolved = & ruby -e "require 'rubygems'; puts File.join(Gem::Specification.find_by_name('try-cli').gem_dir, 'try.rb')" 2>$null |
                Select-Object -First 1
    if ($resolved -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $resolved)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path -Parent $cacheFile) | Out-Null
        Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $resolved -Encoding utf8
        $script:TryScriptPath = $resolved
    }
}
# try-cli gem not installed → stay inert (don't define `try`).
if (-not $script:TryScriptPath) { return }
```

Notes:
- Invalidation matches the zsh `-nt` test: refresh when the ruby binary is newer than the cache (`cache.LastWriteTime >= ruby.LastWriteTime` == fresh). A `mise install ruby@NEW` bumps the binary mtime → auto-refresh. A gem-only `gem update try-cli` that keeps the same ruby won't; note in a comment that `Remove-Item $cacheFile` forces a rebuild (same escape hatch as `98_tv_cache`).
- Env is set *before* the guard so `$env:TRY_PATH` exists even without the tool (cheap, harmless, useful to other tooling) — but the expensive ruby cold start and the `try` definition are gated. If you prefer strict herdr-style "return if not installed," move the two env lines below the guard; I recommend keeping them above.

---

## 3. The `try` function — capture stdout, leave stderr on the console

```powershell
function try {
    if (-not $script:TryScriptPath) { Write-Error 'try: try-cli not resolved'; return }

    # Passthrough sub-commands that must NOT be run through `exec`/translation.
    # (`exec init` would open the selector; `exec --help` leaks a search query.)
    if ($args.Count -ge 1 -and $args[0] -in @('--help','-h','help','--version','version','init')) {
        & ruby $script:TryScriptPath @args   # stdout+stderr straight to console
        return
    }

    # Expected non-zero exit (user cancels selector) must not throw under a
    # `Stop` / $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference=$true profile.
    $prevPref = $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference
    $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false
    try {
        # Assignment captures the SUCCESS stream (stdout) as string[]; the child's
        # STDERR is NOT captured — it flows to the real console, so the selector
        # TUI is visible + interactive (try checks tty on stderr/stdin, which we
        # leave attached; the proven bash wrapper likewise captures stdout).
        $out  = & ruby $script:TryScriptPath exec --path $env:TRY_PATH @args
        $code = $LASTEXITCODE
    }
    finally { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $prevPref }

    $text = ($out -join "`n")

    # Translate ONLY a genuine emit-script: exit 0 AND the stable marker present.
    # Everything else (Cancelled., errors, help/version that slipped through) is
    # echoed verbatim — the robust, version-proof discriminator.
    if ($code -eq 0 -and $text -match "you didn't launch try from an alias") {
        _Try-InvokeEmitted -Emitted $text
    }
    elseif ($text.Trim()) {
        $text -split "`r?`n" | ForEach-Object { Write-Host $_ }
    }
}
```

**Stdout/stderr answer (pwsh 7):** `$out = & ruby …` captures only the success stream; a native command's stderr is written straight to the host and is *not* folded into `$out` (nor turned into `ErrorRecord`s) — exactly the split we want. Two caveats worth writing into the plan:
- **`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` (7.3+):** with `$ErrorActionPreference='Stop'` a non-zero native exit *throws*. The cancel path returns exit 1, so shadow the pref locally (as above). Setting the automatic var when it doesn't exist (7.2) just creates a harmless local.
- **Windows TUI is the one genuinely unverifiable piece off-Windows.** Capturing stdout redirects it to a pipe, but try's interactive selector reads keys in raw mode and draws to stderr; on Unix that's `/dev/tty`, on Windows it's a different console API in Ruby. The *non-interactive* paths (`try . name`, `try <url>`, and test-hook-driven flows) are fully deterministic and translate correctly; the *interactive selector* (`try`, `try <partial>`) can only be smoke-tested on a real Windows box. Call this out as the top residual risk.

---

## 4. The translator

### 4a. POSIX quote tokenizer

```powershell
# Tokenize one POSIX command line: '…' (literal), "…" (kept literal — we never
# need $-expansion because those tokens only occur in specially-handled blobs),
# bare words split on whitespace. Adjacent quoted+bare segments concatenate, so
# the q() escape '\'' (emitted as '"'"') round-trips: 'a'"'"'b' -> a'b.
function _Try-ParsePosixArgs {
    param([string]$Line)
    $tokens = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
    $cur = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new(); $has = $false
    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $Line.Length; $i++) {
        $c = $Line[$i]
        if ($c -eq "'") { $has=$true; $i++; while ($i -lt $Line.Length -and $Line[$i] -ne "'") { [void]$cur.Append($Line[$i]); $i++ } }
        elseif ($c -eq '"') { $has=$true; $i++; while ($i -lt $Line.Length -and $Line[$i] -ne '"') { [void]$cur.Append($Line[$i]); $i++ } }
        elseif ($c -eq ' ' -or $c -eq "`t") { if ($has) { $tokens.Add($cur.ToString()); [void]$cur.Clear(); $has=$false } }
        else { $has=$true; [void]$cur.Append($c) }
    }
    if ($has) { $tokens.Add($cur.ToString()) }
    ,$tokens.ToArray()
}
```

### 4b. Splitter + short-circuit driver

```powershell
function _Try-InvokeEmitted {
    param([string]$Emitted)
    # Drop the leading '#' marker/comment line(s).
    $body = (($Emitted -split "`r?`n") | Where-Object { $_ -notmatch '^\s*#' }) -join "`n"
    if (-not $body.Trim()) { return }   # empty = nothing to do (defensive)

    # Split ONLY at the real element separator: " && \" + newline + indent.
    # The inline " && " inside `test -d 'X' && rm -rf 'X'` lacks the backslash-
    # newline, so it stays one element. (Separator is od-verified: 20 26 26 20 5C 0A.)
    $elements = $body -split ' &&[ ]\\\r?\n\s*'

    foreach ($el in $elements) {
        $cmd = $el.Trim()
        if (-not $cmd) { continue }
        if (-not (_Try-InvokeOne -Command $cmd)) { break }   # && semantics: stop on hard failure
    }
}
```

### 4c. Per-verb dispatch

```powershell
function _Try-InvokeOne {
    param([string]$Command)

    # --- Special compound elements handled before generic tokenizing ---

    # worktree creator: /usr/bin/env sh -c '…worktree add --detach '\''P'\''…'
    if ($Command -match '/usr/bin/env sh -c') {
        $target = if ($Command -match "worktree add --detach '([^']*)'") { $Matches[1] }
        if ($target) {
            $root = if ($Command -match "git -C '([^']*)' rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree") {
                        & git -C $Matches[1] rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>$null
                    } else {
                        & git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>$null   # from live session cwd == try's cwd
                    }
            if ($root) { & git -C $root worktree add --detach $target *> $null }   # `|| true`: non-fatal
        } else { Write-Warning "try: could not parse worktree target in: $Command" }
        return $true   # never abort the chain; the following cd must still run
    }

    # delete restore trailer: ( cd 'PWD' 2>/dev/null || cd "$HOME" )  [1.7.1]
    #                      or  cd 'PWD' 2>/dev/null || cd 'BASE'      [1.9.3 spec]
    if ($Command -match '2>/dev/null\s*\|\|\s*cd') {
        $prim = if ($Command -match "cd '([^']*)'") { $Matches[1] }
        $fallbacks = [regex]::Matches($Command, "cd '([^']*)'") | ForEach-Object { $_.Groups[1].Value }
        $dest = @($fallbacks) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $dest) { $dest = $HOME }   # covers the "$HOME" fallback that has no single-quoted form
        Set-Location -LiteralPath $dest
        return $true
    }

    # delete element: test -d 'X' && rm -rf 'X'   (inline &&, single element)
    if ($Command -match '^\s*test -d ') {
        $left = ($Command -split ' && ', 2)[0]
        $p = (_Try-ParsePosixArgs $left)[-1]
        if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $p) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $p -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
        return $true
    }

    # --- Generic verbs ---
    $t = _Try-ParsePosixArgs $Command
    if ($t.Count -eq 0) { return $true }
    switch ($t[0]) {
        'cd'    { Set-Location -LiteralPath $t[-1]; return $? }
        'mkdir' { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $t[-1] | Out-Null; return $? }
        'echo'  { Write-Host (($t | Select-Object -Skip 1) -join ' '); return $true }
        'touch' {
            # NOT a no-op: try uses dir mtime for MRU ordering in the selector.
            $p = $t[-1]
            if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $p) { (Get-Item -LiteralPath $p).LastWriteTime = Get-Date }
            else { New-Item -ItemType File -Force -Path $p | Out-Null }
            return $true
        }
        'git'   { & git @($t[1..($t.Count-1)]); return ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) }   # clone, worktree move, …
        'mv'    { Move-Item -LiteralPath $t[-2] -Destination $t[-1] -Force; return $? }
        'rm'    { $p=$t[-1]; if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $p) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $p -Recurse -Force }; return $true }
        'ln'    {  # ln -s TARGET LINK  (graduate; needs Dev Mode/admin on Windows → non-fatal)
            try { New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path $t[-1] -Target $t[-2] -Force -ErrorAction Stop | Out-Null }
            catch { Write-Warning "try: symlink skipped (needs Developer Mode/admin): $($t[-1])" }
            return $true
        }
        default { Write-Warning "try: unhandled command, skipped: $Command"; return $true }   # keep the final cd alive
    }
}
```

**`&&` semantics choice:** hard-failure verbs that gate correctness (`mkdir`, `git clone`) return `$false` → abort (so we never `cd` into a dir whose clone failed — matches POSIX `&&`). Best-effort/decorative verbs (`echo`, `touch`, `ln`, `rm`, worktree, restore-cd) and **unknown verbs** return `$true` so the terminal `cd` still runs. That satisfies "keep the final cd working even if an earlier step is unrecognized."

---

## 5. Edge cases & failure modes

- **User cancels selector** → try prints `Cancelled.`, exit 1. Marker absent + non-zero → echoed verbatim, no translation. (Empty stdout with exit 1 → nothing printed.)
- **`try --help` / `--version` / `help` / `init`** → routed to raw `ruby $script @args` (no `exec`, no translation); output streams to console. Anything else that emits non-marker stdout at exit 0 (future subcommands) also falls through the marker gate to verbatim print.
- **Ruby uninstalled mid-session** → the `try` function still exists but `& ruby …` fails; `$LASTEXITCODE` non-zero / native error surfaces. Acceptable; optionally re-check `Get-Command ruby` at the top of `try` and print a friendly hint.
- **try-cli gem removed but ruby present** → resolution at load time yields nothing → fragment returns inert, `try` undefined. Mid-session removal → the cached path fails `Test-Path`; add a `try`-time guard that clears the cache and re-resolves once.
- **Paths with spaces / quotes** → handled by the tokenizer and `-LiteralPath` everywhere. The `'\''` (`'"'"'`) escape round-trips through `_Try-ParsePosixArgs`. Windows paths under `%USERPROFILE%\src\tries` contain spaces (e.g. OneDrive KFM `…\OneDrive\Documents`) — `-LiteralPath` + splatting avoids globbing/quoting bugs.
- **`$env:TRY_PATH` doesn't exist yet** → env normalization uses `GetFullPath` (no existence needed) and only `Resolve-Path`-es if present; the emit's own `mkdir -p` creates it on first use.
- **Symlink privilege (graduate `ln -s`)** → `New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink` needs Developer Mode or elevation on Windows; wrapped in try/catch, non-fatal, warns. (Not emitted by 1.7.1 at all — future-proofing for 1.9.3.)
- **Worktree add into the pre-mkdir'd dir** → git may refuse a non-empty target; the `*> $null` + always-`$true` return mirrors the POSIX `|| true`, then we `cd` into whatever exists.
- **Version drift** → the marker gate + unknown-verb warn-and-continue mean a new verb in 1.9.3+ degrades to "warn + still land in the right dir" rather than crashing. The restore-trailer handler already tolerates both the 1.7.1 subshell/`"$HOME"` form and the 1.9.3 `cd 'BASE'` form by collecting *all* `cd '…'` candidates and picking the first that exists, else `$HOME`.
- **Console encoding** → `00_env.ps1` already sets UTF-8; the selector's box-drawing/ANSI depends on it, so ordering after `00_env` matters (it is).
- **`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` throw** on cancel → shadowed locally (§3).

---

## 6. Pester test strategy

Test file: `tests/Try.Tests.ps1` (matches `tests/GitAliases.Tests.ps1`: `BeforeAll { . $Fragment }`). Because the fragment `return`s early without ruby/try-cli, dot-sourcing it in CI still defines the helpers as long as ruby+try-cli are present on the runner (they are on this Mac) — otherwise gate those `Describe`s with `-Skip:(-not (Get-Command ruby …))`.

**Layer 1 — pure unit (no ruby, hermetic, cross-platform):**
- `_Try-ParsePosixArgs`: `"mkdir -p '/a b/c'"` → `@('mkdir','-p','/a b/c')`; the escape `"cd 'a'\''b'"`→ wait, feed the real emitted form `"cd 'a'`+`'"'"'`+`'b'"` → `@('cd',"a'b")`; empty single-quote `"echo ''"` → `@('echo','')`.
- Splitter: assert a pasted authentic 4-line create emit splits into exactly 4 elements and that `"test -d 'x' && rm -rf 'x'"` stays a **single** element.

**Layer 2 — integration against authentic emit, real side effects (the important layer):** each `It` uses a temp `TRY_PATH`, generates the *real* emit string by calling `ruby $script exec …` (authentic, side-effect-free — `exec` only prints), runs `_Try-InvokeEmitted`, and asserts filesystem + final `Get-Location`. Wrap each in `Push-Location`/`finally { Pop-Location; Remove-Item $tmp -Recurse -Force }`.

- **create**: run generation from a **non-git** temp cwd → deterministic `mkdir_cd`. Assert the dated dir exists and `(Get-Location).Path -eq $expected`.
- **clone**: `git init --bare $tmp/origin.git` (tiny, offline), generate `exec clone "file://$tmp/origin.git"`, translate, assert dest is a git repo and is the final location. (Keeps `& git clone` real without network — Pester can't cleanly mock a native exe, so use a local file:// remote instead of mocking.)
- **worktree**: create a temp git repo with one commit, `Push-Location` into it, generate `exec worktree . wt`, translate, assert a detached worktree dir exists (`git -C … worktree list`) and is the final cwd. Also assert non-fatality: point at a dirty/edge repo and confirm it still `cd`s.
- **delete / rename**: these come only from the selector — generate them **non-interactively via try's own test hooks**: seed the temp `TRY_PATH` with a couple of dated dirs, then `exec --and-keys 'CTRL-D ENTER' --and-confirm y <partial>` (delete) and `exec --and-keys 'CTRL-R' --and-type newname --and-confirm ENTER <partial>` (rename). Assert the dir is gone / renamed and the final location is the restore target (original cwd, else `$HOME`).

**Layer 3 — the `try` gate logic (no filesystem):** mock the ruby call by temporarily shadowing with a `function ruby { … }` in the test scope (or refactor the invocation behind a thin `_Try-RunRuby` seam you can `Mock`). Assert: exit 1 + `Cancelled.` → verbatim, no `Set-Location`; exit 0 + marker → translation invoked; `try --help` → raw passthrough (no `exec`, no translation).

**Not generatable locally:** `ascend`/`graduate` (`ln -s`, `git worktree move`) don't exist in 1.7.1, so their goldens must come from the downloadable 1.9.3 `try.rb` (or hand-written strings). Cover them at Layer 1/2 with checked-in golden strings and assert the `ln`/`git worktree move`/`mv` handlers, marking symlink assertions `-Skip:($IsWindows -and -not <devmode>)`.

Design a PowerShell 7 (pwsh) profile fragment that makes the Ruby CLI `try` (github.com/tobi/try, gem `try-cli` v1.9.3) work on Windows, for a chezmoi dotfiles repo at /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles. Output a concrete design (with pwsh code sketches) + an edge-case/risk list + a Pester test strategy. This is for an implementation plan — do NOT edit files.

## Essential background (already researched — trust this)

`try`'s shell integration works by: the shell wrapper runs `ruby <try.rb> exec --path <TRY_PATH> <args>`, captures stdout, and evaluates it. try-cli 1.9.3 DOES detect pwsh and emit a pwsh wrapper (`$out | Invoke-Expression`), BUT its runtime `exec` output is STILL POSIX shell — the `emit_script`/`script_*` Ruby helpers never branch on shell. PROVEN: `Invoke-Expression` on that output fails in pwsh with "The term '\' is not recognized" because commands are joined with POSIX ` && \`<newline> continuations, and it uses `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `test -d`, `rm -rf`, `ln -s`, `/usr/bin/env sh -c`. So we CANNOT use `try init`'s pwsh wrapper; we must write our OWN pwsh `try` function that runs `ruby try.rb exec` and translates the POSIX output to native pwsh.

### Exact emit format (from try.rb source, verbatim semantics)
- Output starts with a comment line: `# if you can read this, you didn't launch try from an alias. run try --help.`
- `emit_script(cmds)` prints cmds joined by ` && \`<newline>`  (space-amp-amp-space-backslash, then newline, then 2-space indent).
- Paths are POSIX single-quoted via `q(str)` = `"'" + str.gsub("'", %q('"'"')) + "'"` (i.e. `'...'`, with embedded `'` escaped as `'\''`).
- The command lists (cmds arrays) per operation:
  - `script_cd(path)`        => `["touch 'P'", "echo 'P'", "cd 'P'"]`
  - `script_mkdir_cd(path)`  => `["mkdir -p 'P'"] + script_cd`
  - `script_clone(path,uri)` => `["mkdir -p 'P'", "echo 'Using git clone...'", "git clone 'URI' 'P'"] + script_cd`
  - `script_worktree(path,repo)` => `["mkdir -p 'P'", "echo 'Using git worktree...'", "/usr/bin/env sh -c 'if git -C '\''REPO'\'' rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree ...; then repo=$(git -C ... rev-parse --show-toplevel); git -C \"$repo\" worktree add --detach 'P' ...; fi; exit 0'"] + script_cd`   (repo may be omitted -> uses cwd)
  - `script_delete(paths,base)` => `["cd 'BASE'", "test -d 'X' && rm -rf 'X'", ... , "cd 'PWD' 2>/dev/null || cd 'BASE'"]`
  - `script_ascend/graduate`  => `["git worktree move 'SRC' 'DEST'" OR "mv 'SRC' 'DEST'", "ln -s 'DEST' 'SYMLINK'", "echo 'Graduated...'"] + script_cd(dest)`
  - `script_rename(base,old,new)` => `["cd 'BASE'", "mv 'OLD' 'NEW'", "echo 'NEWPATH'", "cd 'NEWPATH'"]`
- The interactive selector TUI (for `try` with no direct match) is drawn to STDERR (bash wrapper uses `2>/dev/tty`); the final cd-script goes to STDOUT. Non-interactive paths: `try . <name>` (mkdir_cd or worktree), `try <git-url>` (clone). Interactive: `try`, `try <partial>` (selector -> cd/delete/rename).

### Required fragment behavior
1. Set `$env:TRY_PATH` (honor existing; default `Join-Path $HOME 'src/tries'`), and `$env:TRY_PROJECTS` = its parent. Normalize like the unix `32_try.zsh` (which does `${TRY_PATH:A}` realpath + `${TRY_PATH:h}` parent).
2. Resolve the try.rb path via `ruby -e "require 'rubygems'; puts File.join(Gem::Specification.find_by_name('try-cli').gem_dir, 'try.rb')"`. This is a ~30-60ms ruby cold start — CACHE it to a file (e.g. `$HOME/.cache/pwsh/try_script.txt`), invalidating when the ruby binary is newer than the cache (mirror the unix mtime-check cache idiom in dot_config/zsh/tools/32_try.zsh lines 16-28). Fragment must be INERT (early `return`) if ruby/try-cli isn't installed (this is an opt-in tool). Model the guard on dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1 line 32.
3. Define `function try { ... }` that runs `ruby <script> exec --path $env:TRY_PATH @args`, letting the child's STDERR go to the real console (so the selector TUI is visible + interactive) while capturing STDOUT, then translate+execute the POSIX stdout natively in the CURRENT pwsh session (so `cd` actually changes the session's location).

### The translator — core design questions to resolve
- How to split try's stdout into logical commands: strip the leading `#` comment line(s); split on the regex for ` && \`<newline><indent> (element separator) — NOT on every ` && ` (because `test -d 'X' && rm -rf 'X'` is a single element with an inline ` && `).
- A POSIX single-quote parser to extract args back to raw strings (handle the `'\''` escape).
- Per-verb dispatch to native pwsh, with correct &&-style short-circuit (stop the sequence if a step fails):
  - `cd 'P'` -> Set-Location -LiteralPath ; `mkdir -p 'P'` -> New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force ; `touch 'P'` -> no-op ; `echo 'MSG'` -> Write-Host ; `git clone 'URI' 'P'` and `git worktree move ...` -> run git verbatim ; `mv` -> Move-Item ; `ln -s 'A' 'B'` -> New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink (note: needs Developer Mode/admin on Windows — may fail, keep non-fatal) ; `test -d 'X' && rm -rf 'X'` -> if (Test-Path -LiteralPath X) { Remove-Item X -Recurse -Force } ; `cd 'D' 2>/dev/null || cd 'B'` -> try D else B.
  - `/usr/bin/env sh -c '...worktree add --detach 'P'...'` -> translate NATIVELY: `$root = git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (from the invocation cwd, or the embedded REPO path); `git -C $root worktree add --detach 'P'` — extract the target path P from the sh -c string. Keep non-fatal (`|| true` semantics).
- Robustness/coupling: this is coupled to try's emit format across versions. Recommend how to make it defensive (e.g., a catch-all: if a command doesn't match a known verb, warn + skip rather than crash; keep the final `cd` working even if an earlier step is unrecognized).

### Deliverables from you
1. A recommended fragment structure (functions: env setup, script-path cache/resolve, `try`, and a private translator like `_Try-InvokeEmitted` + `_Try-ParsePosixArgs`). Give pwsh code sketches for each — especially the splitter, the single-quote arg parser, and the per-verb dispatch with short-circuit.
2. How to capture stdout while leaving stderr on the console in pwsh 7 (e.g. `$out = ruby ... @args` — does native-command stderr pass through to the terminal? note any Windows-specific caveats / the risk that the TUI needs a real tty and this is unverifiable off-Windows). Propose the most robust invocation.
3. Edge cases & failure modes to handle (empty stdout = user cancelled selector; ruby missing mid-session; paths with spaces/quotes; TRY_PATH not existing yet; symlink privilege on Windows; `try --help`/`try init`/other passthrough subcommands that should go straight to ruby without translation).
4. A Pester test strategy: since we can generate REAL emit strings on this mac (ruby + try-cli's newer try.rb is downloadable), how to unit-test `_Try-InvokeEmitted` against authentic try output for create/clone/worktree/delete/rename — asserting side effects (dir created) + final `Get-Location`, using a temp TRY_PATH. Note which paths are non-interactively generatable vs need try's test hooks.

Read dot_config/zsh/tools/32_try.zsh in the UNIX repo at /Users/david/.local/share/chezmoi/dot_config/zsh/tools/32_try.zsh for the caching idiom, and dot_config/powershell/profile.d/25_herdr.ps1 + 98_tv_cache.ps1 in THIS repo for the guard + cache patterns. Be concrete and pwsh-correct.

1	# 32_try.zsh - try-cli configuration (ephemeral workspace manager)
2	# https://github.com/tobi/try

4	# Preserve user overrides, but default the graduate destination to the
5	# normalized parent of TRY_PATH so Ctrl-G has a predictable target.
6	# https://github.com/tobi/try/commit/4bb73376044e1ed8149a66513cec42155bb9f0d3
7	export TRY_PATH="${TRY_PATH:-$HOME/src/tries}"
8	export TRY_PATH="${TRY_PATH:A}"
9	export TRY_PROJECTS="${TRY_PROJECTS:-${TRY_PATH:h}}"
10	
11	# Cached try-cli init: avoids two ruby cold starts (~67ms) on every shell
12	# startup. Mtime check on the ruby binary auto-invalidates after a `mise
13	# install ruby@<NEW>`. After a gem-only upgrade (`gem update try-cli`),
14	# force-refresh with `try-update-completion`. The gem wrapper itself is
15	# broken due to a __FILE__ == $0 guard, hence the indirect ruby try.rb call.
16	_try_cache="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/zsh/try_init.zsh"
17	_try_ruby="$(command -v ruby 2>/dev/null)"
18	if [[ -n "$_try_ruby" ]] && { [[ ! -f "$_try_cache" ]] || [[ "$_try_ruby" -nt "$_try_cache" ]]; }; then
19	    _try_script=$(ruby -e "require 'rubygems'; puts File.join(Gem::Specification.find_by_name('try-cli').gem_dir, 'try.rb')" 2>/dev/null)
20	    if [[ -f "$_try_script" ]]; then
21	        mkdir -p "${_try_cache:h}"
22	        ruby "$_try_script" init > "$_try_cache" 2>/dev/null
23	    fi
24	    unset _try_script
25	fi
26	[[ -s "$_try_cache" ]] || { unset _try_cache _try_ruby; return 0; }
27	source "$_try_cache"
28	unset _try_cache _try_ruby
29	
30	# ── try + sesh integration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
31	# Open a try project and immediately start a sesh coding session.
32	# Usage:
33	#   try-sesh some-project       # fuzzy-match or create, then sesh connect
34	#   try-sesh https://github.com/user/repo  # clone, then sesh connect
35	#
36	# The session name follows sesh's dir_length=2 convention:
37	#   e.g. "tries/2026-04-14-some-project"
38	function try-sesh() {
39	    if ! command -v sesh &>/dev/null; then
40	        echo "try-sesh: sesh not found" >&2
41	        return 1
42	    fi
43	    # Run try, which cd's into the project directory
44	    try "$@" || return $?
45	    # Now connect sesh to the directory try landed in

47	}

1	# 25_herdr.ps1 — herdr workspace layout helpers (PowerShell port of the POSIX
2	# repo's dot_config/shell/24_herdr.sh). herdr's model is Workspace -> Tab -> Pane
3	# and its CLI (`herdr workspace|tab|pane|session …`) is the scripting surface that
4	# replaces tmux's new-session/split-window. This fragment gives the same
5	# muscle-memory commands the parent dotfiles have on Unix:
6	#
7	#   herdr-vibe / hvibe  — parametric multi-agent pack in a new workspace
8	#                         (N agent panes + lazygit tab + nvim tab)
9	#   herdr-code / hcode  — repo-scoped single-agent layout
10	#                         (nvim | agent split + monitor tab)
11	#   herdr-here / hhere  — plain "open a workspace here + attach"
12	#   herdr-root / hroot  — hhere at the git-root
13	#   herdr-mark / hmark  — flag a pane "review-pending" (⭐); hunmark clears it
14	#
15	# PORTING NOTES (why this isn't a line-for-line translation):
16	#   * jq -> ConvertFrom-Json (native; no jq dependency).
17	#   * The Unix helpers emit a *bash* on-exit wrapper (`trap '' INT; …; exec
18	#     $SHELL -l`). The pane shell here is pwsh (config.toml default_shell=pwsh),
19	#     so the wrapper is a pwsh script handed to the pane as
20	#     `pwsh -NoLogo [-NoExit] -EncodedCommand <base64>` — base64 sidesteps all the
21	#     nested-quote escaping that `herdr pane run "<cmd>"` would otherwise need.
22	#   * SpecStory has no Windows CLI (see the package script), so the agent-wrap
23	#     helper degrades to raw passthrough unless `specstory` is actually on PATH.
24	#   * Session targeting uses the modern $env:HERDR_SESSION lever (the Unix file
25	#     predates it and juggles HERDR_SOCKET_PATH by hand).
26	#
27	# Untestable off-Windows: herdr installs via irm|iex and is opt-in, so this can
28	# only be exercised on a Windows box with herdr present. The guard below makes it
29	# a clean no-op everywhere else (same pattern as 35_yazi.ps1).
30	
31	# Skip entirely if herdr isn't installed (opt-in tool, not on every host).
32	if (-not (Get-Command herdr -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { return }
33	
34	# ── Internal helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35	
36	# Parse herdr JSON output; return $null on empty/invalid instead of throwing.
37	function _herdr_json {
38	    param([string]$Text)
39	    if (-not $Text) { return $null }
40	    try { $Text | ConvertFrom-Json -ErrorAction Stop } catch { $null }
41	}
42	
43	# git top-level (forward-slash path even on Windows); empty outside a repo.
44	# $Target, if given, resolves the root as seen from that directory.
45	function _herdr_git_root {
46	    param([string]$Target)
47	    if ($Target) { git -C $Target rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>$null }
48	    else         { git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>$null }
49	}
50	
51	# Workspace-label sanitizer: herdr/tmux forbid '.' and ':'; whitespace too.
52	# '/' is kept (labels like vibe/<repo> use it). Mirrors _sesh_sanitize.
53	function _herdr_sanitize {
54	    param([string]$Text)
55	    $Text -replace '[.:\s]', '-'
56	}
57	
58	# Compose the command for one agent pane: specstory-wrap (known providers) then
59	# fall back to raw. $Mode is auto|never (--no-specstory sets never). On Windows
60	# there is no specstory CLI, so unless it's actually installed we always return
61	# the raw agent — future-proofed for `just specstory-build`.
62	function _herdr_wrap_agent {
63	    param([string]$Agent, [string]$Mode = 'auto')
64	    if ($Mode -eq 'never' -or -not (Get-Command specstory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
65	        if ($Agent) { return $Agent } else { return 'claude' }
66	    }
67	    switch ($Agent) {
68	        ''          { return 'specstory run' }
69	        'specstory' { return 'specstory run' }
70	        { $_ -in 'claude', 'codex', 'cursor', 'droid', 'gemini' } { return "specstory run $Agent" }
71	        default     { return $Agent }
72	    }
73	}
74	
75	# UTF-16LE base64 for `pwsh -EncodedCommand` (no spaces/quotes to escape).
76	function _herdr_encode {
77	    param([string]$Script)
78	    [Convert]::ToBase64String([System.Text.Encoding]::Unicode.GetBytes($Script))
79	}
80	
81	# Wrap an inner command with the post-exit behavior, emitted as a single pane
82	# command string. shell = run once then drop to an interactive prompt (pane
83	# stays); restart = respawn loop; kill = run raw (pane closes on exit). The
84	# try/finally is the pwsh analog of the Unix `trap '' INT`.
85	function _herdr_on_exit_wrap {
86	    param([string]$Inner, [string]$Mode, [string]$Label = 'agent')
87	    switch ($Mode) {
88	        'kill' { return $Inner }
89	        'restart' {
90	            $body = "while (`$true) { $Inner; Write-Host '[$Label exited - respawning in 1s; Ctrl+C to stop]' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Start-Sleep -Seconds 1 }"
91	            return "pwsh -NoLogo -EncodedCommand $(_herdr_encode -Script $body)"
92	        }
93	        default {
94	            # shell (also the catch-all): run once, print a re-run hint, stay in a
95	            # prompt via -NoExit so the pane remains usable.
96	            $body = "try { $Inner } finally { Write-Host `"``n[$Label exited - back in shell. Re-run with: $Inner]`" -ForegroundColor Yellow }"
97	            return "pwsh -NoLogo -NoExit -EncodedCommand $(_herdr_encode -Script $body)"
98	        }
99	    }
100	}
101	
102	# specstory-wrap + on-exit-wrap for one agent pane (label = agent name).
103	function _herdr_agent_cmd {
104	    param([string]$Agent, [string]$Mode, [string]$OnExit)
105	    $inner = _herdr_wrap_agent -Agent $Agent -Mode $Mode
106	    $label = if ($Agent) { $Agent } else { 'agent' }
107	    _herdr_on_exit_wrap -Inner $inner -Mode $OnExit -Label $label
108	}
109	
110	# workspace_id of an existing workspace labeled $Label (else $null). Idempotency.
111	function _herdr_ws_by_label {
112	    param([string]$Label)
113	    $json = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr workspace list 2>$null) | Out-String)
114	    if (-not $json) { return $null }
115	    ($json.result.workspaces | Where-Object { $_.label -eq $Label } | Select-Object -First 1).workspace_id
116	}
117	
118	# Create a labeled tab in workspace $Ws (cwd $Cwd) and run $Command in its root
119	# pane. `tab create` returns the new pane id at .result.root_pane.pane_id.
120	function _herdr_tool_tab {
121	    param([string]$Ws, [string]$Cwd, [string]$Label, [string]$Command)
122	    $json = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr tab create --workspace $Ws --cwd $Cwd --label $Label --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
123	    $pane = if ($json) { $json.result.root_pane.pane_id }
124	    if ($pane) { herdr pane run $pane $Command | Out-Null }
125	}
126	
127	# Resolve the target session NAME. An explicit -Session must exist and be
128	# running (validated via `herdr session list --json`); the caller scopes
129	# $env:HERDR_SESSION around its herdr calls. No arg -> ambient session inside
130	# herdr, else "default". Returns $null (after printing) on a bad explicit name.
131	function _herdr_resolve_session {
132	    param([string]$Want)
133	    if ($Want) {
134	        $json = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr session list --json 2>$null) | Out-String)
135	        $sess = if ($json) { $json.sessions | Where-Object { $_.name -eq $Want } | Select-Object -First 1 }
136	        if ($null -eq $sess) {
137	            Write-Error "session '$Want' not found. Start it with: herdr --session $Want"; return $null
138	        }
139	        if ($sess.running -ne $true) {
140	            Write-Error "session '$Want' is not running. Start it with: herdr --session $Want"; return $null
141	        }
142	        return $Want
143	    }
144	    if ($env:HERDR_SESSION) { return $env:HERDR_SESSION }
145	    return 'default'
146	}
147	
148	# Bring up a client attached to the session when run from OUTSIDE herdr, so the
149	# just-created workspace is visible. Inside herdr ($env:HERDR_ENV set) the focus
150	# calls already moved the live client, so this is a no-op.
151	function _herdr_attach_if_outside {
152	    param([string]$Session = 'default')
153	    if ($env:HERDR_ENV) { return }
154	    if ($Session -eq 'default') { herdr } else { herdr session attach $Session }
155	}
156	
157	# ── hvibe: parametric multi-agent pack ──────────────────────────────────────
158	function herdr-vibe {
159	    $target = ''; $noAttach = $false; $onExit = 'shell'; $specstory = 'auto'; $sessionArg = ''
160	    $nAgents = 0; $nAgentsSet = $false; $agentCli = 'claude'; $agentsCsv = ''; $tabPerAgent = $false
161	    $minWidth = if ($env:HVIBE_MIN_WIDTH) { $env:HVIBE_MIN_WIDTH } else { '80' }
162	    # Stagger between launches so agents sharing a cold-start resource (e.g.
163	    # opencode's single SQLite DB) don't race. Set 0 to launch all at once.
164	    $launchStagger = if ($env:HVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER) { $env:HVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER } else { '0.25' }
165	    $positionals = @()
166	
167	    $i = 0
168	    while ($i -lt $args.Count) {
169	        $a = $args[$i]
170	        if     ($a -eq '-p' -or $a -eq '--path') { $i++; $target = $args[$i] }
171	        elseif ($a -eq '--on-exit')       { $i++; $onExit = $args[$i] }
172	        elseif ($a -eq '--agents')        { $i++; $agentsCsv = $args[$i] }
173	        elseif ($a -eq '--session')       { $i++; $sessionArg = $args[$i] }
174	        elseif ($a -eq '--min-width')     { $i++; $minWidth = $args[$i] }
175	        elseif ($a -eq '--tab-per-agent') { $tabPerAgent = $true }
176	        elseif ($a -eq '--no-specstory')  { $specstory = 'never' }
177	        elseif ($a -eq '--specstory')     { $specstory = 'auto' }
178	        elseif ($a -eq '--no-attach')     { $noAttach = $true }
179	        elseif ($a -eq '-h' -or $a -eq '--help') {
180	            Write-Host @'
181	hvibe — herdr multi-agent pack (herdr analog of svibe)
182	
183	Usage:
184	  hvibe [-p DIR] [--on-exit MODE] [--no-specstory] [--no-attach]
185	        [--min-width COLS] [--tab-per-agent] [--session NAME] [N_AGENTS] [AGENT_CLI]
186	  hvibe [...] --agents A1,A2,A3[,...]
187	
188	Builds a new herdr workspace `vibe/<repo>`:
189	  tab "agents" — N agent panes (side-by-side splits; --tab-per-agent = one tab each)
190	  tab "git"    — lazygit (falls back to `git status`)
191	  tab "edit"   — nvim
192	Idempotent: re-running in the same repo focuses the existing workspace.
193	Requires a git repo (pass -p DIR, or cd into one).
194	
195	Modes for choosing agents:
196	  Homogeneous:   hvibe 3 codex                 → 3 panes all codex
197	  Heterogeneous: hvibe --agents claude,codex   → list length = pane count
198	
199	Agent wrapping (auto; opt out with --no-specstory) applies only if the specstory
200	CLI is installed (it has no Windows build yet), else agents run raw.
201	
202	--on-exit MODE (per pane): shell (default) | kill | restart
203	--min-width COLS (default $env:HVIBE_MIN_WIDTH, else 80) auto-picks N when omitted:
204	  N = clamp(term_width / min-width, 1, 6)
205	$env:HVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER (seconds, default 0.25) delays each pane launch.
206	'@
207	            return
208	        }
209	        elseif ($a -like '-*') { Write-Error "hvibe: unknown flag $a"; return }
210	        else { $positionals += $a }
211	        $i++
212	    }
213	
214	    if ("$minWidth" -notmatch '^\d+$' -or [int]$minWidth -lt 1) {
215	        Write-Error "hvibe: --min-width must be a positive integer (got: $minWidth)"; return
216	    }
217	    if ("$launchStagger" -notmatch '^\d+(\.\d+)?$') {
218	        Write-Error "hvibe: HVIBE_LAUNCH_STAGGER must be a non-negative number (got: $launchStagger)"; return
219	    }
220	    $minWidth = [int]$minWidth
221	    $staggerMs = [int]([double]::Parse($launchStagger, [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture) * 1000)
222	
223	    $termWidth = if ($env:COLUMNS) { [int]$env:COLUMNS }
224	                 elseif ($Host.UI.RawUI.WindowSize.Width) { [int]$Host.UI.RawUI.WindowSize.Width }
225	                 else { 200 }
226	
227	    # Build the agents list (CSV and positional paths are mutually exclusive).
228	    $agents = @()
229	    if ($agentsCsv) {
230	        if ($positionals.Count -gt 0) {
231	            Write-Error "hvibe: cannot combine --agents with positional N_AGENTS/AGENT_CLI."; return
232	        }
233	        $agents = @($agentsCsv -split ',' | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() } | Where-Object { $_ })
234	        if ($agents.Count -eq 0) { Write-Error "hvibe: --agents list is empty."; return }
235	    }
236	    else {
237	        $pos = @($positionals)
238	        if ($pos.Count -gt 0 -and $pos[0] -match '^\d+$') {
239	            $nAgents = [int]$pos[0]; $nAgentsSet = $true
240	            $pos = if ($pos.Count -gt 1) { $pos[1..($pos.Count - 1)] } else { @() }
241	        }
242	        if ($pos.Count -gt 0) { $agentCli = $pos[0] }
243	        if (-not $nAgentsSet) {
244	            # Auto-pick N from terminal width; clamp [1,6] (herdr right-splits
245	            # nest, so many panes get cramped — use --tab-per-agent for more).
246	            $nAgents = [int]($termWidth / $minWidth)
247	            if ($nAgents -lt 1) { $nAgents = 1 }
248	            if ($nAgents -gt 6) { $nAgents = 6 }
249	        }
250	        for ($k = 0; $k -lt $nAgents; $k++) { $agents += $agentCli }
251	    }
252	
253	    if ($onExit -notin 'shell', 'kill', 'restart') {
254	        Write-Error "hvibe: --on-exit must be one of: shell, kill, restart (got: $onExit)"; return
255	    }
256	
257	    $repoRoot = _herdr_git_root -Target $target
258	    if (-not $repoRoot) {
259	        Write-Error "hvibe: not inside a git repo. Pass -p DIR, or cd into a repo."; return
260	    }
261	
262	    # Fail-fast: every agent CLI must exist in PATH.
263	    $missing = @($agents | Where-Object { -not (Get-Command $_ -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) } | Select-Object -Unique)
264	    if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
265	        Write-Error "hvibe: agent CLI(s) not found in PATH: $($missing -join ' ')"
266	        Write-Host  "       Available on Windows: claude codex opencode (install as needed)."
267	        return
268	    }
269	
270	    $repo = Split-Path $repoRoot -Leaf
271	    $label = _herdr_sanitize -Text "vibe/$repo"
272	
273	    $sessName = _herdr_resolve_session -Want $sessionArg
274	    if ($null -eq $sessName) { return }
275	    $hadSession = Test-Path Env:HERDR_SESSION
276	    $prevSession = $env:HERDR_SESSION
277	    if ($sessionArg) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $sessName }
278	    try {
279	        # Idempotent: focus an existing workspace instead of duplicating.
280	        $existing = _herdr_ws_by_label -Label $label
281	        if ($existing) {
282	            Write-Host "hvibe: workspace '$label' already exists ($existing) — focusing."
283	            if (-not $noAttach) {
284	                herdr workspace focus $existing | Out-Null
285	                _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
286	            }
287	            return
288	        }
289	
290	        $wsJson = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr workspace create --cwd $repoRoot --label $label --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
291	        $ws = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.workspace.workspace_id }
292	        $p0 = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.root_pane.pane_id }
293	        $t0 = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.root_pane.tab_id }
294	        if (-not $ws -or -not $p0 -or -not $t0) {
295	            Write-Error "hvibe: failed to create workspace (is the herdr server running?)."; return
296	        }
297	
298	        $firstAgent = $agents[0]
299	        if ($tabPerAgent) {
300	            # One tab per agent; the initial tab hosts the first.
301	            herdr tab rename $t0 $firstAgent | Out-Null
302	            herdr pane run $p0 (_herdr_agent_cmd -Agent $firstAgent -Mode $specstory -OnExit $onExit) | Out-Null
303	            if ($agents.Count -gt 1) {
304	                foreach ($ag in $agents[1..($agents.Count - 1)]) {
305	                    if ($staggerMs -gt 0) { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds $staggerMs }
306	                    _herdr_tool_tab -Ws $ws -Cwd $repoRoot -Label $ag -Command (_herdr_agent_cmd -Agent $ag -Mode $specstory -OnExit $onExit)
307	                }
308	            }
309	        }
310	        else {
311	            # Side-by-side splits in one "agents" tab. Thread $prev so each new
312	            # pane splits off the previous → predictable left-to-right columns.
313	            herdr tab rename $t0 agents | Out-Null
314	            herdr pane run $p0 (_herdr_agent_cmd -Agent $firstAgent -Mode $specstory -OnExit $onExit) | Out-Null
315	            # Even columns: herdr has no even-horizontal layout, so set each
316	            # split's ratio explicitly. --ratio is the fraction KEPT by the pane
317	            # being split; splitting the rightmost pane on step m of N-1 with
318	            # 1/(N-m+1) leaves every finalized column at 1/N width. Format with
319	            # InvariantCulture so a comma-decimal locale never emits "0,5000".
320	            $nTotal = $agents.Count; $prev = $p0; $m = 1
321	            if ($agents.Count -gt 1) {
322	                foreach ($ag in $agents[1..($agents.Count - 1)]) {
323	                    if ($staggerMs -gt 0) { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds $staggerMs }
324	                    $ratio = (1.0 / ($nTotal - $m + 1)).ToString('0.0000', [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)
325	                    $sj = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr pane split $prev --direction right --ratio $ratio --cwd $repoRoot --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
326	                    $np = if ($sj) { $sj.result.pane.pane_id }
327	                    if ($np) {
328	                        herdr pane run $np (_herdr_agent_cmd -Agent $ag -Mode $specstory -OnExit $onExit) | Out-Null
329	                        $prev = $np
330	                    }
331	                    $m++
332	                }
333	            }
334	        }
335	
336	        # git + edit tabs (same on-exit treatment as the agents).
337	        if (Get-Command lazygit -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $gitInner = 'lazygit' } else { $gitInner = 'git status' }
338	        $gitLabel = ($gitInner -split ' ')[0]
339	        _herdr_tool_tab -Ws $ws -Cwd $repoRoot -Label 'git'  -Command (_herdr_on_exit_wrap -Inner $gitInner -Mode $onExit -Label $gitLabel)
340	        _herdr_tool_tab -Ws $ws -Cwd $repoRoot -Label 'edit' -Command (_herdr_on_exit_wrap -Inner 'nvim' -Mode $onExit -Label 'nvim')
341	
342	        if (-not $noAttach) {
343	            herdr workspace focus $ws | Out-Null
344	            herdr tab focus $t0 | Out-Null
345	            _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
346	        }
347	    }
348	    finally {
349	        if ($sessionArg) {
350	            if ($hadSession) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $prevSession }
351	            else { Remove-Item Env:HERDR_SESSION -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
352	        }
353	    }
354	}
355	
356	# ── hcode: repo-scoped single-agent layout ──────────────────────────────────
357	function herdr-code {
358	    $target = ''; $agent = ''; $noAttach = $false; $onExit = 'shell'; $specstory = 'auto'; $sessionArg = ''
359	
360	    $i = 0
361	    while ($i -lt $args.Count) {
362	        $a = $args[$i]
363	        if     ($a -eq '-p' -or $a -eq '--path')  { $i++; $target = $args[$i] }
364	        elseif ($a -eq '-a' -or $a -eq '--agent') { $i++; $agent = $args[$i] }
365	        elseif ($a -eq '--on-exit')      { $i++; $onExit = $args[$i] }
366	        elseif ($a -eq '--session')      { $i++; $sessionArg = $args[$i] }
367	        elseif ($a -eq '--no-specstory') { $specstory = 'never' }
368	        elseif ($a -eq '--specstory')    { $specstory = 'auto' }
369	        elseif ($a -eq '--no-attach')    { $noAttach = $true }
370	        elseif ($a -eq '--agents') {
371	            Write-Error "hcode: --agents is hvibe-only (hcode is single-agent). For multi-agent: hvibe --agents …"; return
372	        }
373	        elseif ($a -eq '-h' -or $a -eq '--help') {
374	            Write-Host @'
375	hcode — herdr single-agent coding layout (herdr analog of scode)
376	
377	Usage: hcode [-p DIR] [-a CLI] [--on-exit MODE] [--no-specstory]
378	             [--no-attach] [--session NAME] [AGENT]
379	
380	Builds a new herdr workspace `coding-agent/<repo>`:
381	  tab "editor"  — nvim (left ~75%) | agent (right ~25%)
382	  tab "monitor" — btop (or htop / a pwsh process view)
383	Idempotent per repo; refuses outside a git repo.
384	
385	Agent wrapping (auto; --no-specstory to opt out) applies only if the specstory
386	CLI is installed (no Windows build yet), else the agent runs raw.
387	--on-exit MODE (per pane): shell (default) | kill | restart
388	
389	  hcode                 # default agent (claude)
390	  hcode codex           # right pane: codex
391	  hcode --on-exit kill claude
392	'@
393	            return
394	        }
395	        elseif ($a -like '-*') { Write-Error "hcode: unknown flag $a"; return }
396	        else { if (-not $agent) { $agent = $a } }
397	        $i++
398	    }
399	
400	    if ($onExit -notin 'shell', 'kill', 'restart') {
401	        Write-Error "hcode: --on-exit must be one of: shell, kill, restart (got: $onExit)"; return
402	    }
403	
404	    $repoRoot = _herdr_git_root -Target $target
405	    if (-not $repoRoot) {
406	        Write-Error "hcode: not inside a git repo. Pass -p DIR, or use hvibe in any dir."; return
407	    }
408	    if ($agent -and -not (Get-Command $agent -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
409	        Write-Error "hcode: agent CLI '$agent' not found in PATH."; return
410	    }
411	
412	    $repo = Split-Path $repoRoot -Leaf
413	    $label = _herdr_sanitize -Text "coding-agent/$repo"
414	
415	    $sessName = _herdr_resolve_session -Want $sessionArg
416	    if ($null -eq $sessName) { return }
417	    $hadSession = Test-Path Env:HERDR_SESSION
418	    $prevSession = $env:HERDR_SESSION
419	    if ($sessionArg) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $sessName }
420	    try {
421	        $existing = _herdr_ws_by_label -Label $label
422	        if ($existing) {
423	            Write-Host "hcode: workspace '$label' already exists ($existing) — focusing."
424	            if (-not $noAttach) {
425	                herdr workspace focus $existing | Out-Null
426	                _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
427	            }
428	            return
429	        }
430	
431	        $wsJson = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr workspace create --cwd $repoRoot --label $label --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
432	        $ws = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.workspace.workspace_id }
433	        $p0 = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.root_pane.pane_id }
434	        $t0 = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.root_pane.tab_id }
435	        if (-not $ws -or -not $p0 -or -not $t0) {
436	            Write-Error "hcode: failed to create workspace (is the herdr server running?)."; return
437	        }
438	
439	        # editor tab: nvim in the initial pane, agent split off right at ~25%.
440	        # --ratio 0.75 keeps nvim (the split pane) at 75%, agent gets 25%.
441	        herdr tab rename $t0 editor | Out-Null
442	        herdr pane run $p0 (_herdr_on_exit_wrap -Inner 'nvim' -Mode $onExit -Label 'nvim') | Out-Null
443	        $sj = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr pane split $p0 --direction right --ratio 0.75 --cwd $repoRoot --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
444	        $ap = if ($sj) { $sj.result.pane.pane_id }
445	        if ($ap) { herdr pane run $ap (_herdr_agent_cmd -Agent $agent -Mode $specstory -OnExit $onExit) | Out-Null }
446	
447	        # monitor tab: btop → htop → a small pwsh process view (no top on Windows).
448	        if     (Get-Command btop -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $monInner = 'btop' }
449	        elseif (Get-Command htop -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $monInner = 'htop' }
450	        else { $monInner = 'while ($true) { Clear-Host; Get-Process | Sort-Object -Property CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 25 -Property Id, ProcessName, CPU, WS | Format-Table -AutoSize | Out-Host; Start-Sleep -Seconds 2 }' }
451	        $monLabel = if ($monInner -like 'while*') { 'monitor' } else { $monInner }
452	        _herdr_tool_tab -Ws $ws -Cwd $repoRoot -Label 'monitor' -Command (_herdr_on_exit_wrap -Inner $monInner -Mode $onExit -Label $monLabel)
453	
454	        if (-not $noAttach) {
455	            herdr workspace focus $ws | Out-Null
456	            herdr tab focus $t0 | Out-Null
457	            if ($ap) { herdr pane focus --direction left --pane $ap | Out-Null }
458	            _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
459	        }
460	    }
461	    finally {
462	        if ($sessionArg) {
463	            if ($hadSession) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $prevSession }
464	            else { Remove-Item Env:HERDR_SESSION -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
465	        }
466	    }
467	}
468	
469	# ── hhere: plain "open a workspace here + attach" ────────────────────────────
470	function herdr-here {
471	    $cmd = ''; $target = ''; $noAttach = $false; $sessionArg = ''
472	    $rest = @()
473	
474	    $i = 0
475	    while ($i -lt $args.Count) {
476	        $a = $args[$i]
477	        if     ($a -eq '-c' -or $a -eq '--command') { $i++; $cmd = $args[$i] }
478	        elseif ($a -eq '-p' -or $a -eq '--path')    { $i++; $target = $args[$i] }
479	        elseif ($a -eq '--session')     { $i++; $sessionArg = $args[$i] }
480	        elseif ($a -eq '--no-attach')   { $noAttach = $true }
481	        elseif ($a -eq '-h' -or $a -eq '--help') {
482	            Write-Host @'
483	hhere — open a plain herdr workspace here + attach (herdr analog of shere)
484	
485	Usage: hhere [-p DIR] [-c CMD] [--no-attach] [--session NAME] [CMD...]
486	
487	Creates a herdr workspace at DIR (default $PWD), focuses it, and — from OUTSIDE
488	herdr — attaches a client. No git repo required and no agent layout: just a shell
489	in the root pane. For agent layouts use hcode (single) or hvibe (multi).
490	
491	  hhere                        # plain shell at $PWD
492	  hhere npm run dev            # bare args → run as the root-pane command
493	  hhere -c "npm run dev"       # explicit --command flag
494	  hhere -p ~/proj              # explicit path
495	Idempotent: re-running in the same dir focuses the existing workspace.
496	'@
497	            return
498	        }
499	        elseif ($a -like '-*') { Write-Error "hhere: unknown flag $a"; return }
500	        else { $rest = $args[$i..($args.Count - 1)]; break }
501	        $i++
502	    }
503	    if ($rest.Count -gt 0 -and -not $cmd) { $cmd = $rest -join ' ' }
504	    if (-not $target) { $target = $PWD.Path }
505	
506	    $label = _herdr_sanitize -Text (Split-Path $target -Leaf)
507	
508	    $sessName = _herdr_resolve_session -Want $sessionArg
509	    if ($null -eq $sessName) { return }
510	    $hadSession = Test-Path Env:HERDR_SESSION
511	    $prevSession = $env:HERDR_SESSION
512	    if ($sessionArg) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $sessName }
513	    try {
514	        $existing = _herdr_ws_by_label -Label $label
515	        if ($existing) {
516	            Write-Host "hhere: workspace '$label' already exists ($existing) — focusing."
517	            if (-not $noAttach) {
518	                herdr workspace focus $existing | Out-Null
519	                _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
520	            }
521	            return
522	        }
523	
524	        $wsJson = _herdr_json -Text ((herdr workspace create --cwd $target --label $label --no-focus 2>$null) | Out-String)
525	        $ws = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.workspace.workspace_id }
526	        $p0 = if ($wsJson) { $wsJson.result.root_pane.pane_id }
527	        if (-not $ws -or -not $p0) {
528	            Write-Error "hhere: failed to create workspace (is the herdr server running?)."; return
529	        }
530	
531	        # Optional command in the root pane (raw — no specstory/on-exit wrapping).
532	        if ($cmd) { herdr pane run $p0 $cmd | Out-Null }
533	
534	        if (-not $noAttach) {
535	            herdr workspace focus $ws | Out-Null
536	            _herdr_attach_if_outside -Session $sessName
537	        }
538	    }
539	    finally {
540	        if ($sessionArg) {
541	            if ($hadSession) { $env:HERDR_SESSION = $prevSession }
542	            else { Remove-Item Env:HERDR_SESSION -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
543	        }
544	    }
545	}
546	
547	# ── hroot: like hhere but at the git-root ────────────────────────────────────
548	function herdr-root {
549	    if ($args.Count -gt 0) {
550	        if ($args[0] -eq '-h' -or $args[0] -eq '--help') {
551	            Write-Host @'
552	hroot — open a plain herdr workspace at the git-root + attach (analog of sroot)
553	
554	Usage: hroot [-c CMD] [--no-attach] [--session NAME] [CMD...]
555	
556	Like hhere, but the workspace opens at the current git top-level (falls back to
557	$PWD outside a repo). All flags except -p/--path pass through to hhere.
558	'@
559	            return
560	        }
561	        if ($args[0] -eq '-p' -or $args[0] -eq '--path') {
562	            Write-Error "hroot: -p/--path is not accepted (root is derived from git). Use hhere -p DIR."; return
563	        }
564	    }
565	    $root = _herdr_git_root
566	    if (-not $root) { $root = $PWD.Path }
567	    herdr-here -p $root @args
568	}
569	
570	# ── Review-pending flag (hmark / hunmark, ⭐) ────────────────────────────────
571	# Toggle a per-pane "I still need to review this" flag via herdr's custom-status
572	# metadata — orthogonal to agent state, so peeking into a done pane does NOT
573	# clear it. Inlined here (the Unix version shells out to a review-mark.sh shared
574	# with a keybind + tv channel; neither exists in this repo yet). Default pane =
575	# ambient $env:HERDR_PANE_ID.
576	function herdr-mark {
577	    if ($args.Count -gt 0 -and ($args[0] -eq '-h' -or $args[0] -eq '--help')) {
578	        Write-Host "hmark [PANE_ID] — flag a herdr pane as review-pending (⭐). Default: current pane."; return
579	    }
580	    $pane = if ($args.Count -gt 0) { $args[0] } else { $env:HERDR_PANE_ID }
581	    if (-not $pane) { Write-Error "hmark: no pane id (not inside herdr?). Pass one: hmark w1:p1"; return }
582	    herdr pane report-metadata $pane --source review --custom-status '⭐ REVIEW' | Out-Null
583	    Write-Host "review flag set on $pane"
584	}
585	
586	function herdr-unmark {
587	    if ($args.Count -gt 0 -and ($args[0] -eq '-h' -or $args[0] -eq '--help')) {
588	        Write-Host "hunmark [PANE_ID] — clear a herdr pane's review-pending (⭐) flag. Default: current pane."; return
589	    }
590	    $pane = if ($args.Count -gt 0) { $args[0] } else { $env:HERDR_PANE_ID }
591	    if (-not $pane) { Write-Error "hunmark: no pane id (not inside herdr?). Pass one: hunmark w1:p1"; return }
592	    herdr pane report-metadata $pane --source review --clear-custom-status | Out-Null
593	    Write-Host "review flag cleared on $pane"
594	}
595	
596	# ── Aliases ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
597	Set-Alias -Name hvibe   -Value herdr-vibe   -Scope Global
598	Set-Alias -Name hcode   -Value herdr-code   -Scope Global
599	Set-Alias -Name hhere   -Value herdr-here   -Scope Global
600	Set-Alias -Name hroot   -Value herdr-root   -Scope Global
601	Set-Alias -Name hmark   -Value herdr-mark   -Scope Global
602	Set-Alias -Name hunmark -Value herdr-unmark -Scope Global
603
```

```text
1	# 98_tv_cache.ps1 — precompute the alias/function list for the `tv aliases`
2	# channel. tv runs channel commands in a fresh shell without our $PROFILE, so we
3	# snapshot the current session's aliases + functions here (runs late, after the
4	# other fragments have defined theirs). No-op if tv isn't installed.
5	if (Get-Command tv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
6	    $dir = Join-Path $HOME '.cache/tv'
7	    $out = Join-Path $dir 'aliases.txt'
8	    # The alias/function set is stable across shells (it's defined by this same
9	    # $PROFILE), so recomputing it on every start is wasted work. Refresh at most
10	    # once a day; delete the file to force an immediate rebuild after adding aliases.
11	    $stale = (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $out)) -or
12	             ((Get-Item -LiteralPath $out).LastWriteTime -lt (Get-Date).AddDays(-1))
13	    if ($stale) {
14	        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null
15	        $lines = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
16	        Get-Alias | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) -> $($_.Definition)") }
17	        Get-Command -CommandType Function -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
18	            Where-Object { -not $_.Source -or $_.Source -eq 'Copilot' } |
19	            Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) (function)") }
20	        $lines | Set-Content -Path $out -Encoding utf8
21	    }
22	}
23

1	# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
2	# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
3	if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
4	    function y {
5	        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
6	        try {
7	            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
8	            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
9	            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
10	                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
11	            }
12	        } finally {
13	            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	        }
15	    }
16	}

```text
1	# 28_tldr.ps1 — tldr language-preference helper.
2	#
3	# Native PowerShell port of the cross-platform dot_config/shell/28_tldr.sh.
4	# `tldrf` runs the tldr client (tlrc, installed via scoop) with a fallback chain
5	# of languages: tlrc accepts `-L` multiple times and tries them in order, so a
6	# page missing in zh_TW falls back to zh, then en. Plain `tldr` keeps its native
7	# behaviour. Override the order in your untracked local.ps1 if you like.
8	
9	# Language preference order for tldrf fallback.
10	$TLDR_LANGUAGES = @('zh_TW', 'zh', 'en')
11	
12	function tldrf {
13	    if (-not (Get-Command tldr -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { return }
14	    $langFlags = foreach ($lang in $TLDR_LANGUAGES) { '-L'; $lang }
15	    & tldr @langFlags @args
16	}
17
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm seeing the actual implementation details now. The wrapper script redirects stderr to the terminal and evaluates stdout on success, otherwise displays it as an error message. The `exec` command can dispatch to different subcommands like `clone`, `worktree`, or `cd`, with a default query operation if none is specified.

The exit code behavior is critical: zero means the script was successfully emitted for evaluation (prefixed with a warning comment), while non-zero means an error or cancellation occurred (plain message output). The PowerShell function needs to check `$LASTEXITCODE` to determine whether to evaluate the output or just print it as-is. Commands like `--help`, `init`, and `version` always go through the `exec` path, so the design should handle those appropriately. So the wrapper runs `ruby try.rb exec`, captures the output and exit code, then checks if it starts with the SCRIPT_WARNING marker — if it does and the exit is 0, translate it; otherwise print the output as-is. This handles help, version, and other passthrough cases elegantly without needing to enumerate them. But I need to think through what happens with `try init` specifically. So the passthrough approach is cleaner: detect a small set of subcommands like `--help`, `-h`, `help`, `--version`, `version`, and `init`, then run ruby directly without the `exec` prefix to avoid the TUI selector confusion. Everything else goes through the `exec` path with marker-based translation. I'm also noticing version drift in the script—the final element handling differs between versions, with one using a subshell and `$HOME` fallback with different quoting styles.

The version differences are exactly why I need to be defensive here: the final restore-cwd step shouldn't try to parse rigidly, but instead treat it as best-effort since the format varies across versions. And for the git clone command, the URI is single-quoted directly rather than going through a quoting function, which works fine since URIs rarely contain single quotes anyway.

For parsing the multi-line command structure, the key is recognizing that commands are joined by ` && \` followed by a newline and indentation—that backslash-newline sequence is the real discriminator, not just any ` && ` substring. So I need to split on that literal pattern to separate the individual commands correctly.

Now I'm checking whether the `/usr/bin/env sh -c '...'` worktree command itself contains embedded `&&` operators that might interfere with the parsing logic. Parsing shell quoting... For the worktree path extraction, I can use a regex to pull P from the sh -c string, then invoke git to get the repository root and add the worktree—handling the current working directory context since the worktree is created from wherever the command runs. The path might contain escaped quotes, but that's unlikely for a date-prefixed directory slug, so I'll accept that edge case as non-fatal.

When `try . name` runs from inside a repo, the script gets called with no explicit repo argument, so git uses the current shell's working directory. But when an explicit path is passed like `try . /path/to/repo name`, the repo is set and git uses the `-C` flag with that path. So I need to check the regex match for `git -C '<R>'` to determine which root to use, falling back to the current working directory if that pattern isn't found.

The worktree add operation needs to complete before the subsequent directory change happens.  Since `mkdir -p` creates an empty directory first and `git worktree add` requires the path to either not exist or be empty, that works fine — and if the worktree add fails, the empty directory remains and we still cd into it, which matches the POSIX behavior.

Now I'm checking how to capture stdout while letting stderr pass through in PowerShell 7 — when I assign a native command's output to a variable, PowerShell captures stdout as an array of strings, but stderr from native commands goes to the error stream by default. So when we capture stdout with `$out = nativecmd`, stderr still flows directly to the terminal—it's not captured or converted to error records. The TUI's selector reads from the actual terminal device and writes to stderr, which means it reaches the console directly without any redirection needed. On Windows there's no `/dev/tty`, but the bash wrapper handles that with stderr redirection, whereas in PowerShell we don't need to redirect at all since stderr naturally goes to the console.

The real concern is whether try.rb's interactive TUI actually works on Windows—it checks for a terminal device and uses raw mode with ANSI codes, which may not be available. The non-interactive paths definitely work, but the selector is the risky part. I should also verify how to capture stdout as a single string instead of an array—using `Out-String` after the command gives me one block of text rather than line-by-line.

For the continuation logic, I need to capture the output with `-join "`n"` to reconstruct the original format (since try emits ` && \` at the end of each line), and immediately grab `$LASTEXITCODE` to preserve the exit code. When PowerShell captures lines as an array, each element includes the trailing ` && \` but not the newline character, so rejoining with "`n" recreates the exact output format.

Now I'm working through the `q()` single-quote escaping in Ruby — the pattern converts a literal single quote into `'"'"'` by using the replacement string that closes the current quote, adds an escaped quote, then reopens.

I need to build a POSIX-compliant argument parser that handles mixed quoting: single-quoted segments (no escapes), double-quoted segments (with escapes), and bare characters, so it can properly parse something like `'a'"'"'b'` into the original token. _**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 10:30:12Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`

```text
1	# Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 — managed by chezmoi (Windows dotfiles).
2	#
3	# Thin loader: dot-sources every fragment in ~/.config/powershell/profile.d in
4	# sorted order, then the user's untracked local override. Keep logic in the
5	# fragments, not here — this file is intentionally boring.
6	
7	$ConfigRoot  = Join-Path $HOME '.config/powershell'
8	$FragmentDir = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'profile.d'
9	$ModuleDir   = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'modules'
10	
11	# Make chezmoi-managed modules importable (e.g. Copilot).
12	if (Test-Path $ModuleDir) {
13	    if (($env:PSModulePath -split [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator) -notcontains $ModuleDir) {
14	        $env:PSModulePath = $ModuleDir + [System.IO.Path]::PathSeparator + $env:PSModulePath
15	    }
16	}
17	
18	if (Test-Path $FragmentDir) {
19	    foreach ($fragment in Get-ChildItem -Path $FragmentDir -Filter '*.ps1' | Sort-Object Name) {
20	        try { . $fragment.FullName }
21	        catch { Write-Warning "profile.d: failed to load $($fragment.Name): $_" }
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	# Untracked, user-local overrides (secrets, machine-specific tweaks).
26	$LocalProfile = Join-Path $ConfigRoot 'local.ps1'
27	if (Test-Path $LocalProfile) { . $LocalProfile }
28
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Pester tests for the git-alias fragment (profile.d/21_git.ps1).
2	#
3	# The functions are thin `git …` wrappers, so we assert the *generated
4	# definitions* rather than running git (unavailable/irrelevant off a real repo).
5	# The things worth locking down: the omz mappings survive edits, gl means pull
6	# (not this repo's old `git log`), gcm/gm stay the PowerShell built-ins the user
7	# asked to keep, and the case-only omz variants (gbD/gcB) fail safe to their
8	# lowercase twin because pwsh command names are case-insensitive.
9	
10	BeforeAll {
11	    $Fragment = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot '..' 'dot_config' 'powershell' 'profile.d' '21_git.ps1'
12	    . $Fragment
13	}
14	
15	Describe 'git alias fragment' {
16	
17	    Context 'static passthroughs map to the oh-my-zsh command' {
18	        It '<Name> -> git <Cmd>' -TestCases @(
19	            @{ Name = 'gst';   Cmd = 'status' }
20	            @{ Name = 'gs';    Cmd = 'status' }              # bonus, non-omz
21	            @{ Name = 'gco';   Cmd = 'checkout' }
22	            @{ Name = 'gcb';   Cmd = 'checkout -b' }
23	            @{ Name = 'gcmsg'; Cmd = 'commit --message' }
24	            @{ Name = 'gcam';  Cmd = 'commit --all --message' }
25	            @{ Name = 'gc';    Cmd = 'commit --verbose' }
26	            @{ Name = 'gp';    Cmd = 'push' }
27	            @{ Name = 'gl';    Cmd = 'pull' }                # omz: gl = pull, NOT log
28	            @{ Name = 'glo';   Cmd = 'log --oneline --decorate' }
29	            @{ Name = 'gaa';   Cmd = 'add --all' }
30	            @{ Name = 'gbd';   Cmd = 'branch --delete' }
31	        ) {
32	            (Get-Command $Name -CommandType Function).Definition.Trim() |
33	                Should -BeExactly "git $Cmd @args"
34	        }
35	    }
36	
37	    Context '!-suffixed amend/force aliases are defined and callable' {
38	        It '<Name> is a function' -TestCases @(
39	            @{ Name = 'gc!' }
40	            @{ Name = 'gca!' }
41	            @{ Name = 'gcann!' }
42	            @{ Name = 'gpf!' }
43	        ) {
44	            Get-Command $Name -CommandType Function | Should -Not -BeNullOrEmpty
45	        }
46	    }
47	
48	    Context 'dynamic (branch-aware) helpers resolve to the right git verb' {
49	        It '<Name> definition matches <Pattern>' -TestCases @(
50	            @{ Name = 'gswm';  Pattern = 'switch \(git_main_branch\)' }
51	            @{ Name = 'gpsup'; Pattern = 'push --set-upstream origin \(git_current_branch\)' }
52	            @{ Name = 'grbom'; Pattern = 'rebase.*origin/.*git_main_branch' }
53	            @{ Name = 'gdup';  Pattern = "diff '@\{upstream\}'" }
54	            @{ Name = 'glol';  Pattern = 'log --graph --pretty=' }
55	        ) {
56	            (Get-Command $Name -CommandType Function).Definition | Should -Match $Pattern
57	        }
58	    }
59	
60	    Context 'PowerShell built-ins we promised to keep' {
61	        It 'gcm stays the Get-Command alias' {
62	            $c = Get-Command gcm
63	            $c.CommandType | Should -Be 'Alias'
64	            $c.Definition  | Should -Be 'Get-Command'
65	        }
66	        It 'gm stays the Get-Member alias' {
67	            $c = Get-Command gm
68	            $c.CommandType | Should -Be 'Alias'
69	            $c.Definition  | Should -Be 'Get-Member'
70	        }
71	    }
72	
73	    Context 'case-only omz variants fail safe (pwsh is case-insensitive)' {
74	        It 'gbD resolves to the non-force gbd' {
75	            (Get-Command gbD -CommandType Function).Definition.Trim() |
76	                Should -BeExactly 'git branch --delete @args'
77	        }
78	        It 'gcB resolves to the -b (not -B) gcb' {
79	            (Get-Command gcB -CommandType Function).Definition.Trim() |
80	                Should -BeExactly 'git checkout -b @args'
81	        }
82	    }
83	
84	    Context 'coverage' {
85	        It 'defines a large git-alias surface' {
86	            (Get-Command -CommandType Function |
87	                Where-Object { $_.Name -like 'g*' -and -not $_.Source }).Count |
88	                Should -BeGreaterThan 150
89	        }
90	    }
91	}
92
```
</details></tool-use>
